reasoning: The Lean formalization captures the informal statement precisely. The predicate `p c` asserts that c is a valid bound for all degree-3 polynomials with a root in [0,1], using `P.degree = 3` (degree exactly 3), `∃ x ∈ Icc 0 1, P.eval x = 0` (root in [0,1]), `∫ x in (0)..1, |P.eval x|` (integral of |P| over [0,1]), and `sSup {y | ∃ x ∈ Icc 0 1, y = |P.eval x|}` (which equals max_{x ∈ [0,1]} |P(x)| since |P| is continuous on compact [0,1]). The conclusion `IsLeast p (5/6)` correctly encodes "smallest constant C": since the set of valid constants is upward-closed, `IsLeast` requires 5/6 to satisfy the inequality AND be a lower bound of all valid constants, which is exactly "smallest C such that the inequality holds for all such P".
